records.public_reputation: Wrote the first widely trusted post-catastrophe licensing doctrine for dangerous study.
Harbor ballads remember him for stealing back tax archives before a corrupt convoy could vanish them.
Iron-masked oath-rider whose methods became the model for lawful shock cavalry in Roaz.
Built the first standardized civic-safe enchantment benches after too many noble workshops exploded.
Priest-smith who fused domination, heat, and duty into an enduring civic liturgy.
Famous for treating warrants, supply timing, and fear display as one battlefield.
Masquewright whose false-fog pageants once hid an evacuation fleet long enough to clear the harbor.
Forge-trance breaker whose rage-state made him the pattern for siege-smashers in early tower consolidation.
Designed the quarry-to-city rail and ventwork chain that turned Roaz law into geography.
Bound lava-flow practice into civic defense during a major vent crisis.
Turned cathedral kitchens and cloth houses into refuge networks when the roads failed.
Smuggled ledgers instead of jewels and became the cautionary tale behind three generations of contract fraud law.
Route legend who treated rumor, weather, and food security as one map.
Formalized corpse stewardship so the dead could be handled lawfully without civic desecration.
Folk-cunning hero said to have cheated three toll lords without ever technically breaking guest-right.

records.canonical_name: Saint Edris of the Unblinking Record
records.public_reputation: A martyr-scholar who died preserving incriminating truth from both magic and politics.
Kept corpse contagion from becoming civic panic by treating the living and ceremonial dead under one doctrine.
First famed road-shield of the present age, remembered for holding the Northroad through a glyph-beast winter.
Oath-bearer judge who escorted fever-dead through panic without ever breaking rite or mercy.
Folk hero archer remembered for breaking a famine raid by signal-shot discipline instead of brute slaughter.
Her confirmed kills mattered less than the terror her sightings created.
Route legend who mapped the first widely trusted flashmelt-safe sled paths.
Distilled volcanic reagents into the first stable fire-salve trusted outside ritual combat.
Inquisitor-marshal credited with early warrant standards for illicit public magic.
Lifted sigil formulae from unsafe private vaults and resold them as corrected safety copies.
